title: Failed to start a container using an image in the additional image stores
body:
### Issue Description

When I create and start a container which created from an image in the additional image stores,
the container failed to start and prints the error message as shown below:

```
Error: unable to start container "<containerId>": crun:
open `.../storage/overlay/<layerId>/merged/run/.containerenv`:
No such file or directory:
OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Make an additional image stores directory and pull an image (root user).

```console
# mkdir /var/lib/containers/share-images

# podman pull --root /var/lib/containers/share-images/ ubi9
... snip ...
e7236a3e070f267713ad79c451b8628166abc0bc9c855f624619e099ec3faa99

# chmod -R a+rx /var/lib/containers/share-images/
```

2. Check an additional image stores is set.

```console
$ cat ${HOME}/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"

[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
        "/var/lib/containers/share-images",
]

$ podman images
REPOSITORY                       TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE        R/O
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9  latest      e7236a3e070f  2 weeks ago  217 MB      true
```

3. Create a container using an image in the additional image stores.

```console
$ podman create --name tests ubi9 sleep inf
5bc8cc504b9102bfb31865e83e3fdae2d6b2022155c551656d50608f5cb893cd
```

4. Start an container.

```console
$ podman start tests
```

### Describe the results you received

The container fail to start.

```console
$ podman start tests
Error: unable to start container "5bc8cc504b9102bfb31865e83e3fdae2d6b2022155c551656d50608f5cb893cd": crun: open `/home/test/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/754a0621b7af510302fa265ba0f1e44e22fcaf9626fe782d09e196a210ccac8c/merged/run/.containerenv`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

### Describe the results you expected

The container success to start.

```console
$ podman start tests
tests
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
the files are not owned by a user in the user namespace, so they appear as user nobody inside the rootless overlay mount.

You need to use fuse-overlayfs if you want to use an additional store owned by root (or any user that is not in the rootless user namespace).

You need to add the following lines to your `storage.conf` file:

```
[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```
